title: corydalis verticillaris subsp. grandiflora
intro: Corydalis verticillaris subsp. grandiflora, also known as the hairy corydalis, is a flowering plant in the poppy family Papaveraceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat a range of conditions including pain, fever, and inflammation due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Corydalis verticillaris subsp. grandiflora include reducing pain and inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a decoction, tincture, or tea, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
